task_completion.description: A mass component representing the camera is added to the main Body Tube.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket component tree (usually on the left), there should be a 'Mass component' nested under the main 'Body Tube'.

task_completion.description: The camera mass component is configured to weigh exactly 150g (0.15 kg).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The configuration window for the camera mass component shows the 'Mass' field set to 150 g or 0.15 kg.

task_completion.description: A ballast mass is added to the Nose Cone and the overall stability margin is adjusted to at least 1.50 calibers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The component tree shows a 'Mass component' nested under the 'Nose Cone'. The stability margin indicator (usually in the top right of the rocket design view) displays a value of 1.50 cal or higher.

task_completion.description: A flight simulation is run and the time-series data is exported to camera_flight.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab shows a completed simulation. An export dialog or a file manager window shows 'camera_flight.csv' saved in the '~/Documents/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: The modified rocket design is saved as camera_retrofitted.ork.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title bar displays 'camera_retrofitted.ork' or a save dialog shows the file being saved to '~/Documents/rockets/camera_retrofitted.ork'.

task_completion.description: An engineering report is created documenting the camera mass, final ballast mass, and final stability margin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'retrofit_report.txt' in '~/Documents/exports/'. The text contains the camera mass (150g), a specific ballast mass value, and a stability margin of at least 1.50.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenRocket application interface (component tree, configuration windows, simulation tabs) rather than just terminal commands or a text editor modifying the .ork XML file directly.

integrity.description: The CSV file was exported using OpenRocket's built-in export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket 'Export data' dialog is visible, rather than the agent manually creating a fake CSV file using a script or text editor.

integrity.description: The engineering report contains specific values derived from the OpenRocket session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ballast mass and stability margin in the text report match the values seen in the OpenRocket GUI screenshots, indicating they were not just guessed or copy-pasted from the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The camera mass must be exactly 150g (0.15 kg) and placed in the Body Tube. The ballast mass must be placed in the Nose Cone. The final stability margin must be >= 1.50 calibers. The exported CSV must be saved to ~/Documents/exports/camera_flight.csv. The modified rocket must be saved to ~/Documents/rockets/camera_retrofitted.ork. The engineering report must be saved to ~/Documents/exports/retrofit_report.txt and contain the camera mass, ballast mass, and stability margin.
